description: This figure presents a set of TEM bright-field images capturing the post-tensile deformed microstructures of the Ni-free Fe-Mn-Al-C austenitic low-density steel designated 28Mn0Ni. Panel (a) shows the microstructure of the HR+CR+973 K sample deformed to a true strain of ε~0.14, revealing dislocation multiplication and cell structure formation within partially recrystallized austenite. Panels (b–e) display the HR+CR+1173 K fully recrystallized condition at increasing tensile strains: (b) at ε~0.02 shows initial planar slip bands; (c) at ε~0.08 exhibits intersecting slip bands; (d) at ε~0.30 demonstrates progressive refinement of slip band spacing; and (e) highlights interactions between dislocations and prior annealing twins. The sequence from (b) to (d) illustrates dynamic slip band refinement with applied strain, while (e) evidences dislocation-twin interaction as a key deformation mechanism. Collectively, these images support the conclusion that the Ni-free 28Mn0Ni alloy develops homogeneous slip band spacing during deformation and that planar slip activity, slip band refinement, and dislocation-twin interactions govern the strain hardening behavior in fully recrystallized austenite.
